summary:
Here is a concise research summary:

This study investigates the use of discrete latent representations in time series classification to enhance explainability. By transforming time series data into compact representations using methods like VQ-VAE and DVAE, the research shows that Explainable AI (XAI) techniques can provide more concise and structured explanations without sacrificing classification performance. A novel metric, Similar Subsequence Accuracy (SSA), is proposed to validate the effectiveness of XAI methods. The findings suggest that discrete latent representations can lead to more interpretable, efficient, and faithful explanations in time series analysis, addressing the challenge of explainability in deep learning models.

key_findings: Discrete latent representations preserve and enhance explainability in time series classification
Applying XAI methods to compressed representations leads to concise and structured explanations
Discrete latent representations retain essential characteristics for classification and offer compact, interpretable, and efficient explanations
